author: @OwlTiny
text: Kids want to blow stuff up!  Parents want them to do so safely.  But heck, when I was a kid, I just wanted to know what was the most explosive, then I seem to remember getting interested in physics.  Pleased the labs coming along.

author: @ianfisher6561
text: Health and safety is NOT  a good thing. It has become too intrusive and is now dumbing us down. Many years ago in a very different time and place I used to go to the local chemist shop and purchase  many fun chemicals. There was no internet or YouTube, but the old school books had many exciting chemical reactions. My friends and I survived all this self imposed danger, we learned and had plenty of fun doing it. Ask yourself why is the suicide rate among young people so high, feeling caged by regulations and restrictions imposed by our elite political class.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Totally agree - but this is today's world - and safe experimentation is a start....

author: @technodrone313
text: Had mr wizard books and stuff as a kid. now i have a 3d printer. you can make scientific tools with it even :)
